# Finca Los Castanos - Complete Information

> Historic coffee plantation in the Valle de Agaete, Gran Canaria, Spain. Europe's only coffee-growing region.

## About

Finca Los Castanos is a working coffee plantation located in the Valle de Agaete on Gran Canaria, Canary Islands, Spain. Owned by two friends from Germany who purchased the plantation in 2022, the finca offers guided coffee tours, holiday apartments, and event hosting in a unique tropical setting surrounded by coffee, avocado, mango, lemon, tangerine, banana, olive, and grape plants.

The plantation spans two hectares with approximately 1,200 coffee plants, producing 400 to 500 kilograms of coffee per year. The primary variety grown is Arabica Typica, with experimental cultivation of Arabica Geisha -- the world's most valuable coffee -- making Finca Los Castanos a pioneer in European coffee cultivation.

## Location & Climate

- Address: Camino de Los Romeros S/N, 35489 Agaete, Las Palmas, Spain
- Coordinates: 28.0810937, -15.6731384
- Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/oZts3DjDY23nveaQ9
- Rating: 4.7/5 (384 reviews on Google)
- Region: Valle de Agaete, Gran Canaria, Canary Islands
- Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/ydfGLsmB8QgGFvtD8

The Valle de Agaete has been growing coffee since the early 19th century and is the last place in Europe where coffee is still cultivated. Gran Canaria geographically belongs to Africa but is politically part of the EU, giving it a unique mild, humid microclimate. Temperatures in the valley stay between 15 and 28 degrees Celsius year-round, with the tall palm trees providing natural wind protection and shade -- ideal conditions for Arabica coffee.

## Coffee Tour

### Overview
A guided experience of approximately 45 minutes walking through the coffee plantation. The tour is led by an expert guide who explains the entire coffee process -- from growing and harvesting to drying and roasting. Visitors walk among the coffee trees, learn about the volcanic microclimate's influence on flavour, and understand what makes cultivation in this unique location special.

The tour ends with a tasting of freshly roasted coffee prepared in front of visitors while explaining its aromas and characteristics. Afterwards, visitors can purchase freshly roasted coffee and local products from the on-site shop.

### Pricing
- Adults: 16 EUR (online) / 18 EUR (on-site)
- Children (5-12 years): 6 EUR (online) / 7.50 EUR (on-site)
- Infants (0-4 years): Free
- Duration: approximately 45 minutes including coffee tasting
- Tour languages: Spanish, English, French

### Booking
Online booking recommended (limited places, discounted price).
- Booking page: https://fincaloscastanos.com/book-coffee-tour

### The Coffee
Experts describe the Cafe de Agaete as wonderfully aromatic, soft and mildly fruity, with an exotic note and a caramel finish -- remarkably low in acidity and bitterness. The extraordinary conditions produce a truly special cup: slowly grown in a mild Mediterranean climate, hand-picked and gently roasted.

### Harvest
Coffee cherries are harvested from April to June. The deep red berries are hand-picked -- slow, careful work. From a bucket of berries weighing up to eight kilograms, approximately one kilogram of coffee beans is produced. After harvesting, the berries dry in the sun, changing colour from red to the familiar coffee brown.

## Holiday Apartments

Two vacation rentals on the plantation:

### Casa Los Castanos
- Capacity: Up to 4 guests
- Rooms: 2 (bedroom + living area)
- Amenities: Full kitchen, bathroom, terrace, WiFi, air conditioning
- Highlights: Modern furnishings, surrounded by tropical garden, direct access to plantation
- Booking: https://fincaloscastanos.com/book

### Cabana en Finca
- Capacity: Up to 2 guests
- Rooms: 1 (bedroom/living)
- Amenities: Kitchenette, bathroom, terrace, WiFi
- Highlights: Cosy cabin setting, private terrace, immersed in nature
- Booking: https://fincaloscastanos.com/book

### Nearby Attractions
- Puerto de las Nieves fishing village (few minutes away)
- Tamadaba Natural Park
- Dedo de Dios rock formation
- Agaete beach

## Events

### Venue Details
- Event tent with outdoor area
- Maximum capacity: 75 guests
- Barrier-free access
- Free parking on site

### Amenities
- Air conditioning and heating
- WiFi
- Modern sanitary facilities
- Tables and seating provided
- Modern sound system

### Catering
Guests can organise their own catering or have it arranged by the finca. Professional artists for musical entertainment can be arranged on request.

### Contact for Events

- Email: info@fincaloscastanos.com

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Coffee Tour
- Q: How long does the coffee tour last? A: Approximately 45 minutes including a coffee tasting.
- Q: What languages are tours offered in? A: Spanish, English, and French.
- Q: Do I need to book in advance? A: Online booking is recommended as places are limited. Online prices are also discounted.
- Q: Is the tour suitable for children? A: Yes, children are welcome. Reduced prices for children, free for infants up to 4 years.
- Q: What is included? A: Guided plantation visit, explanations about coffee cultivation, and coffee tasting.

### Accommodation
- Q: How many guests can the apartments accommodate? A: Casa Los Castanos sleeps up to 4, Cabana en Finca up to 2.
- Q: Is WiFi available? A: Yes, free WiFi in both accommodations.
- Q: Is the finca suitable for families? A: Yes, family-friendly. The plantation is in a rural setting.
- Q: How do I get there? A: Located in the Valle de Agaete, Gran Canaria. Rental car recommended. Free parking available.

### Events
- Q: How many guests does the event tent hold? A: Up to 75 guests.
- Q: Is the venue accessible? A: Yes, barrier-free access.
- Q: Can I bring my own catering? A: Yes, self-catering or finca-organised catering available.

### About the Coffee
- Q: Why can coffee be grown in Europe? A: Gran Canaria's unique microclimate (geographically Africa, politically EU) provides ideal conditions.
- Q: What varieties are grown? A: Mainly Arabica Typica, plus experimental Arabica Geisha.
- Q: When is coffee harvested? A: April to June, hand-picked.
- Q: How much coffee is produced? A: 400-500 kg per year from 1,200 plants on 2 hectares.
